notes: National rail operator of Kazakhstan, holding company over passenger/freight/infrastructure subsidiaries. Quasi-sovereign, ultimately owned by Samruk-Kazyna.

segments.name: Freight (general cargo)
segments.description: Bulk and general freight transportation across Kazakhstan and transit

segments.name: Container transit (Middle Corridor)
segments.description: Trans-Caspian / China-Europe transit container traffic via KTZ network

segments.name: Passenger services
segments.description: Long-distance and suburban passenger trains; subsidized tariffs

segments.name: Infrastructure access fees
segments.description: Track access charges from private operators

segments.name: Other (terminals, repair, services)
segments.description: Terminals, locomotive repair, ancillary B2B services

competitors.name: RZD (Russian Railways)
competitors.notes: Alternative northern corridor; cooperative + competitive depending on geopolitics

competitors.name: Trucking / road freight
competitors.notes: Trucking takes ~30% of domestic ton-km; growing

competitors.name: China Railway International
competitors.notes: Counterpart on China side of Middle Corridor; cooperation partner

competitors.name: Caspian Sea shipping
competitors.notes: Middle Corridor depends on Caspian links - bottleneck and competitor

prior_transformations.name: SAP ERP rollout
prior_transformations.description: Standard SAP implementation across HQ and major subsidiaries
prior_transformations.learnings: Customization-heavy; integration with legacy ASOP-12 freight system never fully achieved

prior_transformations.name: Locomotive fleet renewal
prior_transformations.description: Replace Soviet-era locomotives with GE / Alstom platforms
prior_transformations.learnings: On-time, on-budget for hardware; predictive maintenance promised but not delivered

prior_transformations.name: Container terminal Khorgos
prior_transformations.description: Dry port on China border for Middle Corridor traffic
prior_transformations.learnings: Demand exceeded forecasts after 2022 geopolitical shift; capacity now constrained

prior_transformations.name: Digital booking pilot 2020
prior_transformations.description: Web-based freight booking pilot with corporate clients
prior_transformations.learnings: Clients did not adopt - relationship sales model too entrenched; UX too basic vs internal sales reps

strategic_priorities: Reduce dependence on Russian transit corridor
Modernize freight pricing and yield management
Decarbonization / ESG reporting for European clients
Locomotive predictive maintenance to reduce downtime
pain_points: Fragmented IT landscape across 20+ legacy systems
Passenger segment structurally loss-making (social tariff cap)
Skilled tech talent shortage in regional offices
Cross-border data exchange friction with neighbours (RZD, China Railway)
Limited automated tracking - clients demand visibility we cannot provide reliably
